name: Eastern White Pine
summary: The dominant native pine throughout NEPA and one of the fastest-growing conifers available — 2–3 ft/year in good conditions. Soft, bluish-green needles in groups of five. Provides year-round structural cover and important seed for crossbills and nuthatches.
notes: ⚠️ White Pine Needle Disease (WPND) is epidemic across the Pocono Plateau — high May–July precipitation here drives disease severity. Plant at low density only; never in dense monoculture blocks. Best on ridge tops and slopes with good air movement. Wire cage only — tube heat damages this species.
